title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Call useGlassTabs() somewhere UI using widget.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   # widgets go here )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI(     "main",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE, h3("Overview")),     glassTabPanel("details", "Details", h3("Details"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   active_tab <- glassTabsServer("main") }

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: key rule: use ns() UI, use bare id server.
code: # UI function my_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- NS(id)   tagList(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       ns("tabs"),                          # ns() wraps the id in the UI       glassTabPanel("summary", "Summary", selected = TRUE, h3("Summary")),       glassTabPanel("detail",  "Detail",  h3("Detail"))     )   ) }  # Server function my_server <- function(id) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")    # bare id — no ns() here      observeEvent(active(), {       message("Active tab: ", active())     })   }) }  # App ui     <- fluidPage(my_ui("explorer")) server <- function(input, output, session) my_server("explorer") if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Note: tab value strings must unique — glassTabsUI() error duplicates. Values plain strings; need match labels.
code: # Build panels from data tab_defs <- list(   list(value = "revenue", label = "Revenue"),   list(value = "orders",  label = "Orders"),   list(value = "returns", label = "Returns") )  panels <- lapply(tab_defs, function(t) {   glassTabPanel(t$value, t$label, h3(t$label)) })  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   do.call(glassTabsUI, c(list("metrics", selected = "orders"), panels)) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   active <- glassTabsServer("metrics") }

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: # Switch active tab updateGlassTabsUI(session, "main", "details")  # Hide or show a tab hideGlassTab(session, "main", "admin") showGlassTab(session, "main", "admin")  # Append or remove a tab at runtime appendGlassTab(   session, "main",   glassTabPanel("compare", "Compare", h3("Compare")),   select = TRUE ) removeGlassTab(session, "main", "compare")

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: glassTabsUI(   "main",   glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("A")),   glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("B")),   selected = "a",   wrap = TRUE,   compact = FALSE,      # set TRUE inside dashboard cards for tighter layout   extra_ui = tags$div("Right side UI"),   theme = "light" )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Use compact = TRUE embedding glasstabs inside tight layout bs4Dash::bs4Card(). reduces margins, padding, font size widget overflow card.
code: bs4Card(   title = "Explorer", width = 12,   glassTabsUI(     "tabs",     glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("Content")),     glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("More content")),     compact = TRUE      # reduced spacing for card context   ) )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: glassTabsUI() works inside bs4Dash::tabItem(). Place useGlassTabs() dashboardBody() (anywhere first widget — enough). useGlassTabs() needs appear . Inside module renders bs4Dash layout, call module’s UI function.
code: library(shiny) library(bs4Dash)  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(     bs4SidebarMenu(       bs4SidebarMenuItem("Explorer", tabName = "explorer", icon = icon("table"))     )   ),   body = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),                     # place once, anywhere in the body     bs4TabItems(       bs4TabItem(         tabName = "explorer",         bs4Card(           title = "Data explorer",           width = 12,           glassTabsUI(             "s3_tabs",             glassTabPanel("buckets", "Buckets", selected = TRUE,               p("List of S3 buckets here.")             ),             glassTabPanel("objects", "Objects",               p("Object browser here.")             ),             glassTabPanel("preview", "Preview",               p("File preview here.")             )           )         )       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   active <- glassTabsServer("s3_tabs") }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

code: # Tabs glassTabsUI(   "main",   glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("A")),   theme = glass_tab_theme(     halo_bg = "rgba(251,191,36,0.15)",     tab_active_text = "#fef3c7"   ) )  # Select widgets glassMultiSelect(   "metric", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "dark",     accent_color = "#38bdf8"   ) )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Use glassTabCondition() avoid constructing input key manually:
code: # Instead of: condition = "input['main-active_tab'] === 'details'" conditionalPanel(   condition = glassTabCondition("main", "details"),   p("Only visible on the Details tab.") )  # Inside a module — pass the same id as glassTabsUI(): # glassTabsUI(ns("tabs"), ...) conditionalPanel(   condition = glassTabCondition(NS("mymod")("tabs"), "details"),   p("Only visible on the Details tab.") )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: glasstabs_news()      # prints changelog to the console

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: useGlassTabs() must called UI. module, use ns("tabs") UI pass bare "tabs" glassTabsServer() — ns("tabs"). Passing namespaced id (containing -) produce warning suggested fix. glassTabPanel() values must unique within glassTabsUI() call — duplicates cause error naming offending value. selected glassTabsUI() refers tab value, label. glassSelectValue() returns reactive function, list. glassMultiSelectValue() returns list selected() style(). glassMultiSelect(), selected use choice values, labels. Use compact = TRUE inside bs4Dash cards tight layout reduce widget spacing. add new JS/CSS behavior development, reinstall load_all() retesting.

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glasstabs provides animated Shiny widgets tab navigation selection controls: Tabs: glass-morphism tab navigation bar sliding halo, spring easing, luminous transfer trace tabs Multi-select filter: dropdown filter animated checkboxes, live search, tag-pill display, three checkbox styles Single-select dropdown: animated single-select input optional search clear support widgets work plain fluidPage() bs4Dash. can used together independently. —

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
code: # From CRAN  install.packages("glasstabs")  # From GitHub pak::pak("YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/glasstabs")  # From source devtools::install_local("path/to/glasstabs")

what: The one rule: call useGlassTabs() once
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Every glasstabs app needs exactly one call useGlassTabs() somewhere UI. injects CSS JavaScript proper htmltools dependency.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   # <-- this is all you need   # ... rest of your UI )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glassTabPanel() takes unique value, display label, UI content:
code: glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,   h3("Welcome"),   p("This is the overview pane.") )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Pass panels glassTabsUI() along namespace id:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       shiny::h3("Welcome"),       shiny::p("Start here.")     ),     glassTabPanel("analysis", "Analysis",       shiny::h3("Analysis"),       shiny::p("Your charts go here.")     ),     glassTabPanel("settings", "Settings",       shiny::h3("Settings"),       shiny::p("Configuration options.")     )   ) )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: common pattern using one filters drive content inside tab panes. Pass widget extra_ui place glassFilterTags() inside panes show active multi-select filters removable tag pills.
code: choices <- c(North = "north", South = "south", East = "east", West = "west")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("main",     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(       inputId             = "region",       choices             = choices,       show_style_switcher = FALSE     ),     glassTabPanel("summary", "Summary", selected = TRUE,       shiny::h3("Summary"),       glassFilterTags("region"),        # tag pills appear here       shiny::uiOutput("summary_text")     ),     glassTabPanel("detail", "Detail",       shiny::h3("Detail"),       glassFilterTags("region"),        # same filter, second pane       shiny::tableOutput("detail_table")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    selected_regions <- reactive({     input$region %||% unique(unname(choices))   })    output$summary_text <- renderUI({     shiny::p("Showing data for: ",              shiny::strong(paste(selected_regions(), collapse = ", ")))   })    output$detail_table <- renderTable({     data.frame(Region = selected_regions())   }) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: widgets default "dark". Switch "light" supply custom theme object — case override need:
code: # Built-in light preset glassTabsUI("nav",    theme = "light", ...) glassMultiSelect("f", theme = "light", ...)  # Custom — one field each glassTabsUI("nav",   theme = glass_tab_theme(halo_bg = "rgba(251,191,36,0.15)"),   ... )  glassMultiSelect("f", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(accent_color = "#f59e0b") )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Add wrap = FALSE glass halo positions relative card body rather full-page container. Pair theme = "light":
code: library(bs4Dash) library(glasstabs)  choices <- c(Alpha = "alpha", Beta = "beta", Gamma = "gamma")  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(title = "My App"),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(disable = TRUE),   body    = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),     bs4Card(       title = "Analysis", width = 12,       glassTabsUI("dash",         wrap = FALSE,         theme = "light",         extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(           "f",           choices,           theme = "light",           show_style_switcher = FALSE         ),         glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE,           shiny::p("Overview content.")         ),         glassTabPanel("b", "Detail",           shiny::p("Detail content.")         )       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {} if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Animated Tabs — full reference glassTabsUI(): theming, keyboard nav, multiple instances, server patterns Multi-Select Filter — full reference glassMultiSelect(): checkbox styles, custom hues, tag pills, theming Single-Select Dropdown: search, clearable inputs, explicit “” choices, server-side updates Reference — complete function documentation help(package = "glasstabs")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() animated single-select dropdown Shiny. behaves like standard Shiny input: input$<inputId> stores selected value single character string, NULL nothing selected. supports: optional labels search clear controls built-dark light themes custom theming glass_select_theme() selectable marker styles via check_style server-side updates updateGlassSelect()

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  fruits <- c(   Apple  = "apple",   Banana = "banana",   Cherry = "cherry",   Mango  = "mango" )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: # No selection (default) glassSelect("f", fruits)  # Pre-select one value glassSelect("f", fruits, selected = "banana")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: glassSelect("f", fruits, label = "Choose a fruit")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() supports three selection marker styles: "checkbox" (default) "check-" "filled"
code: glassSelect("f1", fruits, check_style = "checkbox") glassSelect("f2", fruits, check_style = "check-only") glassSelect("f3", fruits, check_style = "filled")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: default trigger dropdown use signature rounded glass corners. Pass shape = "square" crisp, selectize-style corners widget sits flush next native selectizeInput() controls without looking place: shape can also switched runtime server updateGlassSelect(session, "region_square", shape = "square"). See corner shape side side native selectize runGlassExample("square-corners").
code: # Rounded (default) glassSelect("region_rounded", fruits, shape = "rounded")  # Square - matches selectizeInput() glassSelect("region_square", fruits, shape = "square")

what: Width, grouped choices, and disabled options
title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: parity native selectizeInput(), glassSelect() accepts width, grouped choices named list, disabled state: disabled disabled_choices can also toggled runtime updateGlassSelect().
code: # Fixed / fluid width glassSelect("region_w", fruits, width = "240px")  # Grouped choices (selectInput()-style named list) glassSelect("food", list(   Fruit = c(Apple = "apple", Banana = "banana"),   Veg   = c(Carrot = "carrot", Pea = "pea") ))  # Disable the whole widget, or specific options glassSelect("region_d", fruits, disabled = TRUE) glassSelect("region_dc", fruits, disabled_choices = "banana")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: Search enabled default. can also enable clear control. hide search box:
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   searchable = TRUE,   clearable = TRUE ) glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   searchable = FALSE )

what: Server-side search for large choice sets
title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: hundreds thousands choices, set server = TRUE UI register glassSelectServer() server function. browser renders initial slice choices asks Shiny matching choices user types. pattern also works control created dynamically renderUI(). Keep glassSelectServer() call server function inputId.
code: many_choices <- stats::setNames(   sprintf("value-%04d", 1:2000),   sprintf("Choice %04d", 1:2000) )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect(     "pick",     many_choices,     selected = "value-1500",     clearable = TRUE,     server = TRUE,     server_limit = 30   ),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   glassSelectServer("pick", many_choices, session = session, limit = 30)   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: can prepend explicit “” option: useful want UI distinguish : - selection - deliberate “” selection
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   include_all = TRUE,   all_choice_label = "All fruits",   all_choice_value = "__all__" )

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: Read current value directly input$<inputId>:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({   message("Selected: ", if (is.null(input$pick)) "NULL" else input$pick) }) }

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper, use glassSelectValue():
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   pick <- glassSelectValue(input, "pick")    observe({     print(pick())   }) }

what: Updating choices and selection from the server
title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: updateGlassSelect() can update: - available choices - current selection - selection marker style follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices updated without selected, widget keeps current value still exists new set choices.
code: observeEvent(input$filled, {   updateGlassSelect(     session,     "pick",     check_style = "filled"   ) }) ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   actionButton("subset", "Keep first 2 fruits"),   actionButton("banana", "Select banana"),   actionButton("clear", "Clear"),   actionButton("filled", "Use filled style"),   glassSelect("pick", fruits, clearable = TRUE),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick)    observeEvent(input$subset, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       choices = fruits[1:2]     )   })    observeEvent(input$banana, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = "banana"     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = character(0)     )   }) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: glassSelect("f", fruits, theme = "dark") glassSelect("f", fruits, theme = "light")

code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "dark",     accent_color = "#f59e0b"   ) )  glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     bg_color = "#1a0a2e",     border_color = "#a855f7",     text_color = "#ede9fe",     accent_color = "#a855f7"   ) )   #other themes glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  #more themes glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     bg_color = "#ffffff",     border_color = "rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",     text_color = "#111111",     accent_color = "#111111",     label_color = "#111111"   ) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() animated multi-select dropdown Shiny. can used inside glassTabsUI() layout completely Shiny page. widget behaves like standard Shiny input: input$<inputId> stores selected values, input$<inputId>_style stores active indicator style. also supports server-side updates updateGlassMultiSelect().

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  fruits <- c(Apple = "apple",              Banana = "banana",              Cherry = "cherry",             Mango  = "mango",              Peach  = "peach")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: # All selected (default) glassMultiSelect("f", fruits)  # Subset pre-selected glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, selected = c("apple", "cherry"))  # Nothing pre-selected glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, selected = character(0))

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Three indicator styles available via check_style: default, style switcher row shown inside dropdown user can change style runtime. Hide show_style_switcher = FALSE:
code: # Bordered box + animated tick (default) glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "checkbox")  # Tick only — minimal, no box border glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "check-only")  # Solid coloured box — each option gets its own hue glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "filled") glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   check_style         = "check-only",   show_style_switcher = FALSE          # lock the style silently )

what: Custom hues for the filled style
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: check_style = "filled", hues auto-assigned evenly around colour wheel. Override named integer vector HSL hue angles (0-360):
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   check_style = "filled",   hues = c(apple = 0, banana = 50, cherry = 340, mango = 30, peach = 20) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: default trigger dropdown use signature rounded glass corners. Pass shape = "square" crisp, selectize-style corners widget blends layouts built around native selectizeInput() controls: shape can also switched runtime server updateGlassMultiSelect(session, "filters_square", shape = "square"). runGlassExample("square-corners") app shows square shape next native selectize.
code: # Rounded (default) glassMultiSelect("filters_rounded", fruits, shape = "rounded")  # Square - matches selectizeInput() glassMultiSelect("filters_square", fruits, shape = "square")

what: Width, grouped choices, and disabled options
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Like native selectizeInput(), glassMultiSelect() accepts width, grouped choices named list, disabled state: disabled_choices skipped “Select ”, can toggled runtime updateGlassMultiSelect().
code: # Fixed / fluid width glassMultiSelect("filters_w", fruits, width = "100%")  # Grouped choices (selectInput()-style named list) glassMultiSelect("food", list(   Fruit = c(Apple = "apple", Banana = "banana"),   Veg   = c(Carrot = "carrot", Pea = "pea") ))  # Disable the whole widget, or specific options glassMultiSelect("filters_d", fruits, disabled = TRUE) glassMultiSelect("filters_dc", fruits, disabled_choices = "banana")

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: three interactive chrome elements can toggled independently. Defaults TRUE:
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   show_style_switcher = FALSE,   # hide the Check / Box / Fill row   show_select_all     = FALSE,   # hide the "Select all" row   show_clear_all      = FALSE    # hide the "Clear all" footer link )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Place glassFilterTags() container anywhere page show active selections removable tag pills. JS engine keeps sync automatically - clicking x tag deselects option. can place multiple glassFilterTags("cat") containers filter — stay sync.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("cat", fruits, show_style_switcher = FALSE),   glassFilterTags("cat")    # can be placed anywhere, even in a different tab pane )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Read selection directly input$<inputId> style input$<inputId>_style:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    # Direct access   observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(input$pick, collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ",    input$pick_style)   }) }

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper, use glassMultiSelectValue():
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   ms <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "pick")    observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(ms$selected(), collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ", ms$style())   }) }

what: Server-side search for large choice sets
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: large filters, set server = TRUE UI register glassMultiSelectServer() server function. control keeps selected values client rendering current page matching choices. pattern also suitable controls inserted renderUI(). Register glassMultiSelectServer() inputId, newly rendered control receive search results Shiny.
code: many_choices <- stats::setNames(   sprintf("value-%04d", 1:2000),   sprintf("Choice %04d", 1:2000) )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect(     "pick",     many_choices,     server = TRUE,     server_limit = 30,     show_style_switcher = FALSE   ),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   glassMultiSelectServer("pick", many_choices, session = session, limit = 30)   output$out <- renderPrint(length(input$pick)) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Updating choices and selection from the server
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: updateGlassMultiSelect() can update: - available choices - current selection - active style follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices updated without selected, widget keeps intersection current selection new choice set.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   actionButton("subset", "Keep first 3 fruits"),   actionButton("pick2", "Select 2 fruits"),   actionButton("clear", "Clear"),   actionButton("fill", "Switch to filled style"),   glassMultiSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick)    observeEvent(input$subset, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       choices = fruits[1:3]     )   })    observeEvent(input$pick2, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = c("apple", "cherry")     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = character(0)     )   })    observeEvent(input$fill, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       check_style = "filled"     )   }) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, theme = "dark")   # default glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, theme = "light")  # white panel, dark text

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Supply colors want change:
code: # One field — accent colour only glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(accent_color = "#f59e0b") )  # Two fields glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     text_color   = "#1e293b",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  # All four fields glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     bg_color     = "#1a0a2e",     border_color = "#a855f7",     text_color   = "#ede9fe",     accent_color = "#a855f7"   ) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() fully independent tab widget. need useGlassTabs() CSS JS:
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  sales <- data.frame(   region  = c("North","North","South","South","East","West"),   product = c("Alpha","Beta","Alpha","Gamma","Beta","Alpha"),   revenue = c(42000, 31000, 27000, 55000, 38000, 61000) )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("region",  c(North="North", South="South",                                 East="East",   West="West"),                    show_style_switcher = FALSE),   glassMultiSelect("product", c(Alpha="Alpha", Beta="Beta", Gamma="Gamma"),                    show_style_switcher = FALSE,                    check_style = "check-only"),   glassFilterTags("region"),   glassFilterTags("product"),   tableOutput("tbl") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   filtered <- reactive({     sales[sales$region  %in% (input$region  %||% unique(sales$region)) &           sales$product %in% (input$product %||% unique(sales$product)), ]   })   output$tbl <- renderTable(filtered()) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() scoped inputId works independently. one dropdown open automatically floats others:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("a", c(X = "x", Y = "y", Z = "z")),   glassMultiSelect("b", c(P = "p", Q = "q", R = "r"),                    check_style = "filled",                    show_style_switcher = FALSE) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: glassTabsUI() provides animated glass-morphism tab navigation bar Shiny. active tab tracked sliding glass halo spring easing, luminous transfer trace sweeps across intermediate tabs navigation.

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Three functions work together: useGlassTabs() — injects CSS JS , anywhere page UI glassTabsUI() — renders full tab bar content area glassTabPanel() — defines one tab button associated content pane
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       h3("Overview"),       p("This pane is shown first.")     ),     glassTabPanel("details", "Details",       h3("Details"),       p("Switch to this tab using the button above.")     ),     glassTabPanel("settings", "Settings",       h3("Settings"),       p("A third tab.")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Reading the active tab in the server
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: active tab value pushed Shiny automatically every click via Shiny.setInputValue. Access input[["<id>-active_tab"]]:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({     req(input[["nav-active_tab"]])     message("Active tab: ", input[["nav-active_tab"]])   })    # Or use the convenience wrapper   active <- glassTabsServer("nav")   observe(message("Active: ", active())) }

code: library(bs4Dash) library(glasstabs)  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(title = "My App"),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(disable = TRUE),   body    = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),     bs4Card(       title = "Analysis", width = 12,       glassTabsUI("dash",         wrap     = FALSE,         theme    = "light",         extra_ui = glassMultiSelect("f", choices, theme = "light",                                     show_style_switcher = FALSE),         glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE, p("Overview content")),         glassTabPanel("b", "Details",  p("Detail content"))       )     )   ) )

title: glasstabs - Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Note: useGlassTabs() must called somewhere UI glassTabsUI() glassMultiSelect() call. injects shared CSS JavaScript properly deduplicated htmltools dependency.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI(     "main",     glassTabPanel(       "t1", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       h3("Overview"),       p("Content here."),       glassFilterTags("cat")     ),     glassTabPanel(       "t2", "Details",       h3("Details"),       p("More content."),       glassFilterTags("cat")     ),     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(       "cat",       c(A = "a", B = "b", C = "c"),       show_style_switcher = FALSE     )   ),   verbatimTextOutput("info") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   tabs <- glassTabsServer("main")   filt <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "cat")    output$info <- renderPrint({     list(       active_tab = tabs(),       selected = filt$selected(),       style = filt$style()     )   }) }  if (interactive()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Animated glass multi-select dropdown filter — glassMultiSelect
text: inputId Shiny input id. choices Named unnamed character vector choices. selected Initially selected values. Defaults choices NULL. label Optional field label shown widget. placeholder Trigger label nothing selected. all_label Label shown choices selected. check_style One "checkbox" (default), "check-", "filled". show_style_switcher Show Check / Box / Fill switcher row inside dropdown? Default TRUE. show_select_all Show "Select " row? Default TRUE. show_clear_all Show "Clear " footer link? Default TRUE. theme Color theme. One "dark" (default) "light", glass_select_theme() object. shape Corner style trigger dropdown. One "rounded" (default) signature glass look, "square" crisp, selectize-style corners widget sits neatly alongside native Shiny selectizeInput() controls. width Optional widget width passed shiny::validateCssUnit(), e.g. 100% 240px. NULL (default) trigger keeps intrinsic width. disabled Logical. TRUE whole widget greyed non-interactive. Default FALSE. disabled_choices Optional character vector choice values render disabled (non-selectable) rows. Default NULL. hues Optional named integer vector HSL hue angles (0 360) "filled" style. Auto-assigned NULL. dark_selector Optional CSS selector signals dark mode (e.g. "body.dark-mode" bs4Dash). provided theme = "light", emits extra scoped <style> block reverts colors dark-mode defaults whenever selector active. server Logical. TRUE, render initial slice choices use glassMultiSelectServer() search full choice set Shiny server. Default FALSE. server_limit Maximum number choices rendered initially returned server-side search. Default 50. server_min_chars Minimum search characters required server-side matching filters choices. Default 0.

title: Animated glass single-select dropdown — glassSelect
text: inputId Shiny input id. choices Named unnamed character vector choices. selected Initially selected value. Default FALSE. disabled_choices Optional character vector choice values render disabled (non-selectable) rows. Default NULL. server Logical. title: Server logic for glass tabs — glassTabsServer
code: # --- Standalone app --- if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       "tabs",       glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),       glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))     )   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")     observe(print(active()))   }   shinyApp(ui, server) }  # --- Bookmarking --- if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- function(request) {     fluidPage(       useGlassTabs(),       bookmarkButton(),       glassTabsUI(         "tabs",         glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),         glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))       )     )   }   server <- function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs", bookmark = TRUE)   }   shinyApp(ui, server, enableBookmarking = "url") }  # --- Inside a Shiny module --- # UI side: use ns() to namespace the widget id my_module_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id)   shiny::tagList(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       ns("tabs"),                          # <-- ns() wraps the id here       glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),       glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))     )   ) }  # Server side: pass the bare id — NOT ns("tabs") my_module_server <- function(id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")     # <-- bare id, no ns()     shiny::observe(print(active()))   }) }

title: glasstabs 0.3.1
text: Error messages across functions now actionable: name bad argument class, show received, suggest correct fix. glass_tab_theme() glass_select_theme() now full @examples interactive app snippets. .gt-container longer forces max-width:960px margin:48px auto — widget now behaves like normal block element inside Shiny layouts. title: glasstabs 0.3.0
text: Added runGlassExample() launch built-example apps directly R console (runGlassExample("smoke-test"), etc.). Added icon argument glassTabPanel() — accepts htmltools-compatible tag (e.g. shiny::icon("table")). Renders gt-tab-icon / gt-tab-label spans icon text can styled independently. Added disableGlassTab() enableGlassTab() — gray tab without hiding . Completes visibility/disabled/removal trio alongside showGlassTab() / hideGlassTab(). Added updateGlassTabBadge() — set numeric count badge tab button. Pass count = 0 count = NA hide badge. Values 99 display "99+". glassTabsServer() now integrates Shiny’s URL bookmarking: active tab automatically saved restored enableBookmarking = "url" set app. Opt per widget bookmark = FALSE. Added glassTabsOutput() / renderGlassTabs() server-driven tab rendering. JavaScript engine re-initialises automatically render via scoped shiny:value listener, manual glasstabs_reinit calls needed.

title: glasstabs 0.3.0
text: glassTabsUI() now errors early duplicate glassTabPanel() values, reporting offending value(s) name. glassTabsUI() gains compact = FALSE argument. Setting compact = TRUE applies .gt-compact CSS modifier reduces margins, tab-link padding, font size, content area padding — ideal embedding inside bs4Dash cards tight dashboard layout. glassTabsServer() emits warning() id argument contains "-", common sign ns("tabs") passed instead bare "tabs" id. warning includes corrective guidance. CSS: added .gt-tab-disabled, .gt-tab-icon, .gt-tab-label, .gt-tab-badge rules. Badges adapt background active halo colour via existing CSS custom property. CSS: color values now consumed via var(--gt-xxx, fallback) instead hardcoded — root cause light theme appearing invisible. Light theme defaults also strengthened better contrast. CSS: dropdown active-state z-index raised 9000/9001 glasstabs dropdowns render bs4Dash card stacking contexts. shiny:value listener limits bootAll re-initialisation outputs actually contain glasstabs elements, ordinary Shiny outputs unaffected. Added inst/cheatsheet/glasstabs-cheatsheet.tex — printable four-column LaTeX reference card covering full API, module pattern, bs4Dash integration, theming, common gotchas.
